pr.title: Split text_3 and work on making large fighter file splits
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that split the monolithic asm/text_3.s and related text blobs into separate fighter assembly files and additional text sections. The PR body states the intent was to split ft*.o files in large chunks rather than creating every ft*special*.o file immediately. It added per-fighter asm files such as asm/ftclink.s, asm/ftdrmario.s, asm/fticeclimber.s, asm/ftkoopa.s, asm/ftluigi.s, asm/ftmars.s, asm/ftmewtwo.s, asm/ftness.s, asm/ftpeach.s, asm/ftpikachu.s, asm/ftpurin.s, asm/ftsamus.s, asm/ftyoshi.s, and asm/ftzelda.s, while moving very large ranges out of asm/text_3.s into asm/text_4.s and the new asm/text_5.s. obj_files.mk was updated to include the new object split layout.
what_changed: Reduced asm/text_3.s substantially, with 206383 deleted lines and 165 added lines across 73 hunks.
Added asm/text_5.s as a very large new assembly text section with 284956 added lines.
Rebalanced asm/text_4.s heavily, with 147485 added lines and 284952 deleted lines.

Created large per-fighter assembly files for many fighter object chunks, including Yoshi, Ness, Jigglypuff/Purin, Ice Climbers, Pikachu, Mewtwo, Peach, Bowser/Koopa, Samus, Marth/Mars, Zelda, Luigi, Young Link/CLink, and Dr. Mario.
Updated obj_files.mk with 16 added lines and 1 deleted line so the build knew about the new split assembly object files.

The diff excerpt shows new fighter files use the existing raw assembly format: .include "macros.inc", .section .text, .global labels such as func_80148B78, and address-preserving comments for instructions.
smart_moves: Used coarse fighter-level ft*.o splits instead of attempting many smaller ft*special*.o splits at once, reducing coordination overhead while still making the monolithic text assembly more navigable.
Kept unmapped code in assembly with original func_/lbl_ symbol names and address comments, preserving matching/build stability while improving file organization.
Updated the object list in the same PR as the file movement, which is essential for large asm splits because file extraction without build-list changes would break or omit objects.
Separated fighter chunks into names that correspond to recognizable fighter abbreviations, making later targeted decompilation easier to search and stage.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic text_N.s becomes too large to work with, a mechanical split into object- or subsystem-sized asm files is valuable even before functions are decompiled to C.
Large assembly moves should preserve labels, instruction comments, section directives, and include directives exactly so future matching work has stable anchors.
Coarse-grained splits can be a practical intermediate step: they do not require perfect final source-file boundaries, but they make subsequent per-character or per-special-action decomp work easier.
For fighter code, file names using the ft prefix plus internal character abbreviation provide useful wayfinding for future reverse engineering.
Build metadata such as obj_files.mk must be treated as part of the split, not an afterthought.
naming_conventions: New fighter assembly chunks use lowercase asm/ft*.s filenames.
The PR uses established fighter abbreviations such as ftclink, ftdrmario, fticeclimber, ftkoopa, ftluigi, ftmars, ftmewtwo, ftness, ftpeach, ftpikachu, ftpurin, ftsamus, ftyoshi, and ftzelda.
Unidentified functions and labels remain in the project’s address-derived placeholder style, for example func_80148B78 and lbl_80148C48.
Assembly files retain macros.inc inclusion and .section .text declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical assembly relocation from monolithic text files into new asm/ft*.s files while preserving original instruction bytes, addresses, and labels.
Use of large chunk splits rather than fine-grained special-move object splits as an incremental organization tactic.
Preserved raw PowerPC assembly with .global symbols and address comments, enabling unchanged references from existing branch/call targets.
Added obj_files.mk entries alongside the new asm files so the linker/object layout could continue to represent the split code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text says: "Splits out the ft*.o files in large chunks, rather than doing each ft*special*.o for now".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/ftclink.s or other new ft*.s chunks from this split into C.
Search obj_files.mk history after PR #45 to see how the coarse ft*.o splits were later refined.
Check whether asm/text_5.s was a temporary overflow split or became a stable boundary for later decompilation.
Look for later naming migrations from ftmars to Marth-specific source names and from ftpurin/ftkoopa to localized fighter names.
